jokes.text: It was opportunity knocking. / No one knocked. You just barged in. / Knock, knock!
jokes.setup: Character claims it was 'opportunity knocking' after barging in
jokes.punchline: Literally performs a knock after being called out
jokes.explanation: Sharp three-beat structure with literal interpretation of idiom creates solid laugh

jokes.text: You remember Donald, my son who's two years older than me.
jokes.setup: Normal introduction of a son
jokes.punchline: The son is older than the father
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan absurdism - mathematically impossible family relationship delivered casually

jokes.text: As I recall, you own the Tracy Jordan Institute for Black Karate.
jokes.setup: Formal recollection of a business venture
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous specificity of 'Black Karate'
jokes.explanation: Absurd business name with racial specificity that sounds both legitimate and ridiculous

jokes.text: You wouldn't believe this, but that business failed. / I blame Obama.
jokes.setup: Admission that the absurd karate business failed
jokes.punchline: Blaming Obama for a clearly doomed venture
jokes.explanation: Standard political blame deflection joke, competent but predictable

jokes.text: NASCAR's Fat Load Café is a gold mine.
jokes.setup: Discussing theme restaurant business models
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous restaurant name 'Fat Load Café'
jokes.explanation: Gross-out name that sounds plausible for NASCAR theming, efficient wordplay

jokes.text: Señor Mexico, hola. Sí, sí. Yo soy Donald, sí. ¿ Ãndale, ándale? ¿ Arriba, arriba?
jokes.setup: Donald pretends to take a call from a Mexican billionaire
jokes.punchline: Uses only Speedy Gonzales catchphrases as Spanish
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - fake phone call using cartoon Spanish reveals character's desperation and ignorance

jokes.text: Jenna and I are mirroring until we achieve touchless orgasm. / And... finished.
jokes.setup: Explanation of bizarre spiritual/sexual practice
jokes.punchline: Immediate completion with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept with perfect timing - introduces bizarre relationship dynamic efficiently

jokes.text: I might as well be working at a roller-skating drag-queen restaurant under Taliban rule!
jokes.setup: Complaining about wearing name tags at work
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and escalating comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna over-the-top comparison with perfect specificity and escalation

jokes.text: And I will say yes when Paul proposes... that we make a sex tape and leak it on the Internet. / Oh. I thought you meant marriage.
jokes.setup: Romantic buildup about saying yes to a proposal
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation - not marriage but sex tape
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that subverts romantic expectations with Jenna's shallow priorities

jokes.text: God, no! Marriage is like death. You settle into a routine, you lose all the spark.
jokes.setup: Liz's assumption about marriage proposal
jokes.punchline: Marriage compared to death with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Dark comparison delivered with Jenna's characteristic shallow wisdom

jokes.text: No, relationships are like sharks, Liz. If you're not left with several bite marks after intercourse, then something's wrong.
jokes.setup: Philosophical statement about relationships being like sharks
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation involving actual bite marks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna logic - takes shark metaphor to disturbing literal conclusion with perfect delivery

jokes.text: Because we are not sharks. We are legless turtles rotting on the beach.
jokes.setup: Response to Jenna's shark relationship philosophy
jokes.punchline: Extremely depressing and specific counter-metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon self-deprecation with vivid, disturbing imagery that's both funny and sad

jokes.text: That's an excellent question. The answer is questions like that.
jokes.setup: Liz asks what makes guys get bored in relationships
jokes.punchline: Jack's response is self-referentially critical
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical Jack Donaghy response - condescending while being helpful

jokes.text: I believe that, when you have a problem, you talk it over with your priest or your tailor or the mute elevator porter at your men's club.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his approach to therapy
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending with 'mute elevator porter'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack elitism with perfect escalation to absurdist conclusion

jokes.text: Then you take that problem and crush it with your mind vise.
jokes.setup: Jack's therapy philosophy continues
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous metaphor of mental 'mind vise'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy pseudo-wisdom with memorable absurd imagery

jokes.text: But for lesser beings, like curly-haired men and people who need glasses, therapy can help.
jokes.setup: Jack conceding therapy might work for others
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and arbitrary categories of 'lesser beings'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack elitism with hilariously specific and random categories of inferiority

jokes.text: Because, and I mean this, I'm tired of talking this much to a woman I'm not having sex with.
jokes.setup: Jack expressing concern for Liz's wellbeing
jokes.punchline: Reveals selfish motivation with casual misogyny
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy honesty that undercuts his apparent kindness

jokes.text: Why did you choose that name? / Because 'staples' means 'the basics.' / Staples is also a giant office-supply chain. / Well, we'll see who's still in business 200 years from now.
jokes.setup: Jack pointing out obvious trademark infringement
jokes.punchline: Donald's delusional confidence about outlasting Staples
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Donald's business incompetence and delusion

jokes.text: Donald, I'm going to pick a problem at random. Is the theme of your restaurant Not Enough Tables?
jokes.setup: Jack surveying the obviously problematic restaurant
jokes.punchline: Suggesting lack of tables might be intentional theme
jokes.explanation: Sharp deadpan observation that treats obvious problem as potential creative choice

jokes.text: Well, I took that model and replaced the knights with unlicensed versions of Japanese monsters.
jokes.setup: Explanation of restaurant concept based on Medieval Times
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of using 'unlicensed versions'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the copyright infringement joke to come, shows Donald's obliviousness

jokes.text: Witness the primeval might that is Godzila, with one 'L' for trademark reasons!
jokes.setup: Dramatic introduction of the monster
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging copyright infringement within the performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about copyright infringement delivered within the fictional performance

jokes.text: Donald, George punched my crotch! / You liked it!
jokes.setup: Complaint about inappropriate physical contact during show
jokes.punchline: Donald's inappropriate response suggesting enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Crude physical comedy that shows the restaurant's dysfunction but relies on cheap shock

jokes.text: Stewart, Brenda, Amber, Crystal. Also, in the background, I heard lady giggles and the sound of a beautiful sunset.
jokes.setup: Kenneth relaying message about Carol's flight crew
jokes.punchline: Claiming to hear the sound of a sunset
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdism - impossibility delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: everyone I ever dated in high school turned out to be either gay or a girl dressed as a guy to get a journalism scholarship.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her trust issues with men
jokes.punchline: Escalating reveal ending with absurdly specific scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from common experience to absurdly specific Liz Lemon backstory

jokes.text: When I was seven, I asked for a CB radio for Christmas so I could track gas prices around the state for a cool chart I was making.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her childhood Christmas wish
jokes.punchline: Extremely nerdy and specific childhood interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - reveals Liz's lifelong nerdiness with specific absurd hobby

jokes.text: You're a bigger disappointment than our current president, Jimmy Carter!
jokes.setup: Young Liz confronting mall Santa
jokes.punchline: Extremely sophisticated political reference from a child
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - shows young Liz was already politically aware and verbose

jokes.text: I am a fraud, little boy! My wife still thinks I work at the bank!
jokes.setup: Santa being accused of fraud
jokes.punchline: Santa admits to actual fraud in his personal life
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - takes metaphorical accusation and makes it literally true

jokes.text: Miss Lemon, there's a reason God gave us two ears and only one mouth. Listening is twice as important as talking.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering wisdom about listening
jokes.punchline: Takes the metaphor literally with mathematical precision
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment taking folk wisdom to literal mathematical conclusion

jokes.text: But He gave us ten fingers. He must really want us to poke things! Poke, poke, poke!
jokes.setup: Following Kenneth's body-parts logic
jokes.punchline: Takes it to absurd conclusion with physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that extends Kenneth's logic to ridiculous conclusion with physical comedy

jokes.text: What about Brown and Folderson? / That's what I call my wallet!
jokes.setup: Jack asking about mentioned investors
jokes.punchline: Tracy admits they're his wallet personified
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that subverts expectation completely - fake investors are just wallet contents

jokes.text: Come on. The boy's only 43 years old.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Tracy needs to cut off Donald financially
jokes.punchline: Referring to 43-year-old son as too young for independence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic treating middle-aged man as child, consistent with earlier age joke

jokes.text: For example, curly-haired men and people who need glasses.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining who's not cut out for business
jokes.punchline: Repeats his earlier arbitrary categories
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Jack prejudices, reinforces his bizarre consistency

jokes.text: And I bankrolled that, too! Thank God we tested it with a monkey first!
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning Donald flying with his own wings
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tracy literally funded wing-flying experiment with monkey testing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation taking metaphor literally with dark implications about animal testing

jokes.text: So far, I have 8 'nos' and 25 'shove it up your goon-holes.'
jokes.setup: Kenneth reporting on softball team signup
jokes.punchline: Specific counts including creative insult category
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth precision in cataloguing rejections with amusing made-up insult

jokes.text: Here's a fun game! Put on Harry's cologne and give me a back rub in the bath!
jokes.setup: Aunt Linda trying to bond with young Liz
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate request involving ex-husband roleplay
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe comedy showing traumatic childhood experience that explains Liz's issues

jokes.text: Oh, my God. My trust issues and my food issues are connected! Uncle Harold is the reason eggs make me gag!
jokes.setup: Liz having therapeutic breakthrough
jokes.punchline: Connecting random psychological dots to egg aversion
jokes.explanation: Good therapy parody showing oversimplified psychological connections

jokes.text: Kenneth, you're the perfect therapist. I can dump all of my problems on you, walk away, and move on with my life! It's a win-win!
jokes.setup: Liz praising Kenneth as therapist
jokes.punchline: Describes exploitative one-sided arrangement as 'win-win'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's oblivious selfishness masquerading as insight

jokes.text: Why did she have to say 'Harold'?
jokes.setup: Kenneth's internal reaction to hearing the name Harold
jokes.punchline: Ominous response suggesting personal connection
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Kenneth's pig story, creates intrigue with minimal dialogue

jokes.text: He said they live in a 'soo-borb'? / Suburb. Come on, Jenna.
jokes.setup: Jenna mispronouncing 'suburb'
jokes.punchline: Shows her complete unfamiliarity with normal life
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character work showing her disconnection from normal American life

jokes.text: Call me old-fashioned, but I think that's the man's job.
jokes.setup: Jenna saying she'll have to propose the sex tape herself
jokes.punchline: Applies traditional gender roles to completely non-traditional situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony applying conservative values to sex tape proposal

jokes.text: I know it means this conversation is disgusting.
jokes.setup: Liz excitedly sharing her breakthrough about eating eggs
jokes.punchline: Jack's disgusted reaction to the topic
jokes.explanation: Good Jack response showing his discomfort with oversharing

jokes.text: Tracy, do not laugh at the word 'duty.'
jokes.setup: Jack about to use the word 'duty' in serious context
jokes.punchline: Has to preemptively warn Tracy about childish reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Jack's knowledge of Tracy's immaturity

jokes.text: For the love of God, stop calling him 'Daddy.'
jokes.setup: Emotional scene between Tracy and Donald
jokes.punchline: Jack's discomfort with inappropriate father-son dynamic
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction to the weird dynamic, Jack as voice of audience discomfort

jokes.text: I'm gonna call it Microsoft.
jokes.setup: Donald describing new business idea combining brewery and frozen yogurt
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated name that's already a huge corporation
jokes.explanation: Good Donald incompetence - suggests naming restaurant after tech giant

jokes.text: Look at his head shape. He has no brain pan!
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Kenneth can't handle therapy
jokes.punchline: Specific cranial insult with made-up term
jokes.explanation: Good Jack elitism with specific physical insult using pseudo-scientific term

jokes.text: And so am I! I'm Cheryl.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Kenneth is okay after trauma
jokes.punchline: Kenneth claims to be fine while clearly having identity confusion
jokes.explanation: Good indication of Kenneth's mental break with efficient identity confusion

jokes.text: You dump your problems on some half-baked Barney Fife, and you can start a chain reaction of mental anguish.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the danger of amateur therapy
jokes.punchline: Specific reference comparing Kenneth to bumbling deputy
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that captures both Kenneth's incompetence and good intentions

jokes.text: Put your mental burden in my mind vise, and I will crush it.
jokes.setup: Jack offering to help Liz with therapy
jokes.punchline: Returns to his earlier absurd 'mind vise' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jack's earlier therapy philosophy with consistent absurd imagery

jokes.text: Please let Harold be human. / Harold was a pig.
jokes.setup: Jack's desperate hope for normalcy
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's matter-of-fact revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Jack's dread immediately justified

jokes.text: But I needed $300 for the river ferry-train-oxcart-train-bus ticket.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining how he got to New York
jokes.punchline: Absurdly primitive and circuitous transportation method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth backstory detail suggesting he's from another era entirely

jokes.text: Why, I once ate an entire witch. A pig was nothing!
jokes.setup: Kenneth confident about winning eating contest
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to eating a witch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth mythology - casually mentions supernatural cannibalism as confidence booster

jokes.text: I would have recognized those eyes anywhere.
jokes.setup: Kenneth about to eat his contest pig
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's his beloved pet Harold
jokes.explanation: Devastating emotional beat delivered with Kenneth's innocent tone

jokes.text: It was Harold, and I ate all of him... even the face, in case of a tie.
jokes.setup: Kenneth recognizing Harold in the eating contest
jokes.punchline: Methodical consumption including disturbing competitive detail
jokes.explanation: Horrifically dark comedy with Kenneth's matter-of-fact delivery making it even more disturbing

jokes.text: His sacrifice made you what you are today, which is... the lowest-level employee at the last-place network in America.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to give Kenneth's story positive meaning
jokes.punchline: Realizes Kenneth's current position undercuts the inspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment realizing his motivational speech backfired

jokes.text: During Nude Hour, I got custard all over my penis.
jokes.setup: Paul casually mentioning work at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific workplace hazard
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy but relies somewhat on shock value

jokes.text: I mean, come on, why can't we just paint each other's toenails, watch vintage pornography, and then go to bed in our swing like a normal couple?
jokes.setup: Paul defending their current relationship activities
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly bizarre activities as 'normal'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how bizarre their 'normal' relationship actually is

jokes.text: Well, there's a sex resort in Japan where white people are treated like slaves.
jokes.setup: Paul asking what Jenna would want after sex tape
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and disturbing next escalation
jokes.explanation: Dark Jenna escalation that's disturbing but shows her relentless need for extreme experiences

jokes.text: I'm saying adopting a dog so it can watch us make love and then returning it, claiming that it bit our imaginary child, is everything that I need.
jokes.setup: Paul trying to express contentment with current relationship
jokes.punchline: Describes elaborate, disturbing scheme as his ideal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation showing Paul's version of romantic satisfaction

jokes.text: I'll trust you to fairly divide up our panties.
jokes.setup: Paul and Jenna breaking up
jokes.punchline: Reveals they share underwear as normal relationship detail
jokes.explanation: Good relationship detail that shows how unusual their dynamic was

jokes.text: Look how small my head is. / Oh, my! It's so tiny!
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on her head size
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's amazed confirmation
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that probably works better on screen than in transcript

jokes.text: Too late. Look how we're positioned.
jokes.setup: Jack telling Tracy not to turn his back on Donald
jokes.punchline: Tracy takes it literally about physical positioning
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy literal interpretation of metaphorical advice

jokes.text: Well, looky here. Doctor Jack, the boy who wants to be a scientist. Chowderhead can't even say the words. You'll never be nothin'!
jokes.setup: Jack's father catching him practicing for science play
jokes.punchline: Cruel mockery with specific insult 'chowderhead'
jokes.explanation: Effective backstory showing origin of Jack's issues with specific regional insult

jokes.text: I am a protoin! Protein! All living orgasms... I mean orgasms!
jokes.setup: Young Jack trying to perform in science play
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces both 'protein' and 'organisms'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing young Jack's stage fright and mispronunciation

jokes.text: says they're beautiful even when they're ugly, thinks they're smart even when they go to Arizona State.
jokes.setup: Jack describing what parents should do
jokes.punchline: Casual dig at Arizona State University
jokes.explanation: Standard college joke showing Jack's elitism, but well-integrated into emotional speech

jokes.text: That's a nice long speech and all, but your daddy was right. You couldn't be a scientist. You couldn't even be protein in a school play.
jokes.setup: Jack's inspirational speech about parental support
jokes.punchline: Donald completely undermines it by agreeing with Jack's abusive father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected redemption - Donald doubles down on cruelty

jokes.text: I... am a protein! All living organisms need me to function! A basic building block of the human body, I am made from amino acids found in ribosomes.
jokes.setup: Jack triggered by Donald's mockery
jokes.punchline: Finally delivers perfect protein speech 30+ years later
jokes.explanation: Brilliant emotional payoff - Jack finally conquers childhood trauma with perfect delivery

jokes.text: Proteins give energy to everything from flowers and butterflies to heroes who turn in Communists.
jokes.setup: Jack continuing his protein speech
jokes.punchline: Escalates from nature to Cold War patriotism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack escalation mixing science education with political ideology

jokes.text: Daddy, can I have $50,000 to start a business where people can call in and get air-quality reports from all across the United States? I'm gonna call it... American Airlines!
jokes.setup: Donald asking for money for new business idea
jokes.punchline: Names it after existing major airline company
jokes.explanation: Good Donald consistency - still completely unaware of trademark issues

jokes.text: This is what happens when you work at being happy! Godzilla sits next to you while you're eating an egg-salad sandwich.
jokes.setup: Liz reflecting on her improved mental state
jokes.punchline: Describes surreal happiness with specific visual
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback integrating Liz's egg breakthrough with the restaurant storyline

jokes.text: I've got no real skills, a degree in Theater Tech, so you move back to Pennsylvania, live with your parents. All of your old friends have kids and careers, and what do you have? Maybe your name on a TV show that no one will even remember.
jokes.setup: Person in Godzilla costume having existential crisis
jokes.punchline: Describes exact situation of struggling TV performer, breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the struggling performer's life with implicit self-reference to the show itself

jokes.text: Damn you, Gojira!
jokes.setup: Performer's breakdown continues
jokes.punchline: Uses original Japanese name while in knockoff costume
jokes.explanation: Nice touch using original name while breaking down in copyright-infringing costume
